summary: Replaced two hardcoded PowerPC address constructions in assembly with symbolic relocations, and exported the missing data label needed by one of them. The changes kept the emitted instructions/comments aligned while making references to lbl_803F2ED0 and lbl_803EECE0 relocatable and searchable by symbol rather than raw address/immediate.
what_changed: Added a global label declaration for lbl_803F2ED0 in asm/data.s immediately before a table beginning with .4byte 0x000000A1.
In asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s at lbl_8027ABAC, replaced lis r3, 0x803f with lis r3, lbl_803F2ED0@ha.
In asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s, replaced addic. r4, r3, 0x2ed0 with addic. r4, r3, lbl_803F2ED0@l.
In asm/melee/mn/mnvibration.s at func_802480B4, replaced lis r6, 0x803f with lis r6, lbl_803EECE0@ha.
In asm/melee/mn/mnvibration.s, replaced addi r30, r6, -4896 with addi r30, r6, lbl_803EECE0@l.
smart_moves: Converted raw high/low address materialization into @ha/@l symbol relocations while preserving the original instruction opcodes shown in the assembly comments.
Handled the negative low half case for lbl_803EECE0 by using lbl_803EECE0@l instead of retaining the opaque signed immediate -4896.
Added the missing .global label for lbl_803F2ED0 at the data location before changing code references to use it, avoiding an unresolved-symbol style fix.
decomp_lessons: When assembly constructs a known data address with lis plus addi/addic, prefer symbol@ha and symbol@l over hardcoded high and low immediates.
If a code reference needs to become symbolic, verify the destination data address has an actual label and global visibility where required.
Signed low-half immediates such as -4896 can correspond to a symbol low relocation; replacing them with @l makes the pointer intent clear without changing the generated machine code.
Small asm-only pointer cleanups improve future C decompilation by tying callsites and tables to named labels instead of raw addresses.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data labels use the lbl_XXXXXXXX address-based convention, as seen with lbl_803F2ED0 and lbl_803EECE0.
Unidentified functions and local branch labels retain address-based names such as func_802480B4, func_8018841C, lbl_8027ABAC, lbl_8027ABCC, lbl_8027ABD4, and lbl_8027ABDC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use PowerPC relocation syntax symbol@ha for the high adjusted half of an address loaded by lis.
Use symbol@l for the low half consumed by addi or addic.
Preserve instruction form and register allocation when replacing constants with relocations, e.g. lis r3 plus addic. r4,r3 and lis r6 plus addi r30,r6.
Symbolizing hardcoded pointers can be a no-codegen-change match tactic when the relocation resolves to the same encoded high and low immediates.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining asm references using raw lis 0x803f or other 0x80xx high-half constants that should be symbol@ha relocations.
Search for addi/addic low immediates paired with hardcoded lis instructions that can be converted to symbol@l.
Audit asm/data.s for unlabeled data blocks that are referenced by code via hardcoded pointers.
